orphan_directories.reason: No package.json — contains only static brand assets (mark-*.png, mark*.svg). Matched by packages/* glob but is not a functional workspace member.
No package.json — appears to contain documentation files only. Matched by packages/* glob but is not a functional workspace member.

slack.name: @opencode-ai/slack
slack.notes: Slack bot integration. Minimal — single index.ts entry. Only package with .env.example.

http-recorder.name: @opencode-ai/http-recorder
http-recorder.notes: Effect-based HTTP recording library for tests. 9 source files. Uses @effect/language-service tsconfig plugin.

containers.name: @opencode-ai/containers
containers.notes: STUB PACKAGE — no src/ directory, zero dependencies. Appears to be a placeholder for future container/Docker functionality.

enterprise.name: @opencode-ai/enterprise
enterprise.notes: Enterprise/team management SolidJS SSR app (Vite + Nitro). Depends on @opencode-ai/core for share/storage utilities.

script.name: @opencode-ai/script
script.notes: Build/infra scripts. Imported by opencode main package. No README.

web.name: @opencode-ai/web
web.notes: Astro-based documentation/marketing site. Uses opencode (unscoped) as devDep for type imports of Session/MessageV2.

console/app.name: @opencode-ai/console-app
console/app.notes: Main console web app (SolidJS SSR). Complex build pipeline involving sitemap generation + vite build + schema generation. Depends on console-core/console-mail/console-resource.

console/core.name: @opencode-ai/console-core
console/core.notes: Backend core for console — Drizzle ORM, Stripe billing, PlanetScale DB, AWS. Uses jsxImportSource: 'react' (for @jsx-email/render). Heavy DB management scripts (drizzle-kit, model promotion, limits). No README.

console/function.name: @opencode-ai/console-function
console/function.notes: Cloudflare Workers functions — AI SDK integration for auth, log processing, stats. Uses jsxImportSource: 'react' anomaly like console/core. No README.

console/mail.name: @opencode-ai/console-mail
console/mail.notes: JSX email templates package. No src/ — uses emails/templates/ directory. No tsconfig.json despite having @tsconfig/bun in deps. Exports map to emails/templates/*.

console/resource.name: @opencode-ai/console-resource
console/resource.notes: Platform resource abstraction with conditional exports (Cloudflare vs Node). No src/ — source files at package root. No README.

console/support.name: @opencode-ai/console-support
console/support.notes: Support portal SolidJS app. Depends on console-core for lookup functionality. No README.

identity.notes: NOT A PACKAGE — static brand assets only. No package.json. Contains: mark-512x512.png, mark.svg, mark-96x96.png, mark-light.svg, mark-512x512-light.png, mark-192x192.png.

plugin.name: @opencode-ai/plugin
plugin.notes: ONLY package with a build step (tsc → dist/). Public plugin SDK. 4 subpath exports. Has optional peer deps on @opentui. files: ['dist']. No README.

function.name: @opencode-ai/function
function.notes: GitHub App Cloudflare Worker — Octokit-based API. Single src/api.ts. No README.

llm.name: @opencode-ai/llm
llm.notes: RICEST exports map (22 entries). 10 LLM providers + 6 protocol adapters. Uses Effect. http-recorder used in dev for test recordings. Uses @effect/language-service plugin.

imported_by.purpose: devDependency — type imports for Session, MessageV2 in Share components and pages

consistency_findings.detail: packages/containers has no src/ directory, zero dependencies, version 1.0.0. Appears to be a placeholder stub for future container/Docker functionality. Has a tsconfig.json and README.md but no actual code.

packages/identity has no package.json. Contains only static brand assets (PNG/SVG logos). Matched by packages/* workspace glob but cannot function as a workspace member. Should either be moved out of packages/ or given a package.json if it's meant to be published.
packages/docs has no package.json. Matched by packages/* glob. Should be moved out of packages/ or made into a proper package.
console/mail has @tsconfig/bun in devDependencies but no tsconfig.json file exists. Also has @types/react and react in deps but uses solid-js in package.json — likely used for @jsx-email templates.

console/core tsconfig uses jsxImportSource: 'react' despite not being a React package. This is for @jsx-email/render which uses React JSX for server-side email rendering. Same pattern in console/function. Consider documenting this or using a dedicated tsconfig for email files only.
Same jsxImportSource: 'react' anomaly as console/core. Used for email template rendering via @jsx-email.
packages/slack is listed explicitly in the workspaces array but is already covered by the packages/* glob. Can be removed without changing behavior.
packages/slack has private: false (implied by omission) but is an internal integration package. Should likely be private: true unless it's meant for external consumption.
packages/script has private: false (implied) but is an internal build-script package. Likely should be private: true.
packages/web has private: false (implied) but is the docs site. Should likely be private: true.

exports field usage varies wildly: llm has 22 subpath exports, plugin has 4, http-recorder and script have simple ones, most packages have none. No consistent pattern for when to use exports. Consider standardizing.
7 of 16 packages have README.md (slack, http-recorder, containers, enterprise, web, console/app, llm). 9 do not. Consider requiring README for all packages.

Three different tsconfig strategies: @tsconfig/bun (5 packages), @tsconfig/node22 (5 packages), standalone/no-extends (3 packages — all Vite/SolidJS frontends). This is somewhat justified by runtime targets but creates inconsistency in compiler options.

plugin is the only package with a build step (tsc → dist/). All other packages rely on Bun's native TypeScript execution or Vite/Astro build pipelines. This is intentional (plugin is published to npm) but worth noting as a pattern break.

No package uses TypeScript project references (references field in tsconfig.json). With 16+ packages and cross-dependencies, project references could improve typecheck performance and enforce build ordering.
Only slack has an .env.example file. Other integration packages (function, llm, web) that touch external APIs lack environment documentation.

common_patterns.description: Most packages use 'tsgo --noEmit' for typechecking (11 of 16). Exceptions: web (astro check), console/mail (none), identity (N/A), containers (typecheck script exists but no src).
Internal dependencies use 'workspace:*' protocol (33 instances across 15 package.json files). Consistent.
Shared dependency versions centralized in root package.json catalog field. Consistent across all packages.
Packages that provide a public API use the exports field in package.json to define entry points. Used by 6 packages: http-recorder, script, console/core, console/mail, console/resource, plugin, llm.
Three packages share identical frontend structure: standalone tsconfig with SolidJS/Vite, noEmit: true, path alias ~/*, src/ with app.tsx, entry-client.tsx, entry-server.tsx, global.d.ts.
Several packages target Cloudflare Workers with @tsconfig/node22, @cloudflare/workers-types, and bundler module resolution.
Three packages use the Effect library (@effect/*) as a core dependency for functional effect management.

anomalies.description: packages/containers is a fully configured workspace member with tsconfig, README, and scripts — but no source code at all.
anomalies.recommendation: Either implement the package or remove it from the workspace.

anomalies.description: packages/identity is matched by the packages/* glob but has no package.json — it's a static assets directory. This causes unnecessary filesystem scanning during workspace resolution.
anomalies.recommendation: Move brand assets out of packages/ or add a minimal package.json with private: true.

anomalies.description: packages/docs is matched by packages/* but is not a package. Similar issue to identity.
anomalies.recommendation: Move out of packages/ or add a package.json.

anomalies.description: Has @tsconfig/bun in devDeps but no tsconfig.json. JSX email templates likely don't need one, but it's inconsistent.
anomalies.recommendation: Either remove the unused devDep or add a tsconfig.

anomalies.description: console/core and console/function use jsxImportSource: 'react' solely for @jsx-email/render server-side email rendering. The rest of the codebase uses solid-js. This is a necessary workaround but should be documented.
anomalies.recommendation: Add a comment in tsconfig.json explaining the React JSX usage for email templates.

anomalies.description: Only plugin ships compiled output via tsc. All other packages rely on runtime transpilation (Bun, Vite, Astro). This is by design but creates a build-specialization pattern.
anomalies.recommendation: Accept as intentional. Document in plugin README.

cross_references_matrix.description: Rows = importing package, Columns = imported package. ✓ = runtime dependency, (d) = devDependency only.
